goal: Add the Week-3 ucil-core Salsa incremental-engine skeleton (dependency DAG, invalidation, early cutoff) and the ucil-daemon plugin-manager skeleton (plugin.toml discovery, process spawning, tools/list MCP health check).
plan_summary: Introduce crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs backed by the `salsa` crate (v0.22+) wrapping a minimal query DAG with at least one tracked input (file mtime), one tracked function, and an invalidation test that exercises early cutoff. Independently introduce crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs that parses plugin.toml into a typed PluginManifest, spawns the plugin binary via tokio::process::Command over stdio, performs an MCP tools/list JSON-RPC health-check with tokio::time::timeout, and returns PluginHealth { name, tools, status }. Wire both modules from their respective lib.rs re-exports; cover both with real unit + integration tests — no mocked salsa, no mocked child process.
scope_in: Add `salsa = "0.22"` (or newest 0.22.x) to crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml; add `toml = "0.8"` to crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml if not already present.

crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs — public module with a #[salsa::db] or #[salsa::jar] skeleton database, at least one #[salsa::input] (e.g. FileRevision { path: PathBuf, mtime: SystemTime }) and one #[salsa::tracked] fn (e.g. symbol_count) demonstrating the dependency DAG.

Unit tests under incremental:: that prove: (a) two calls with the same input are memoised, (b) changing the input's mtime invalidates the tracked fn and it recomputes, (c) if the tracked fn's *result* is unchanged after recompute, a second-order tracked fn's previous result is reused (early cutoff).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — public module with: PluginManifest struct (subset of §14.1 plugin.toml: [plugin], [transport]), PluginManifest::from_path(path) parsing via the `toml` crate, PluginManager::discover(plugins_dir) walking *.toml manifests, PluginManager::spawn(manifest) using tokio::process::Command with stdin/stdout piped, PluginManager::health_check(manifest) sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 tools/list request over the child's stdio and parsing the response with tokio::time::timeout (named const HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000).

Integration tests under plugin_manager:: that launch a real in-tree mock plugin binary (new `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/support/mock_mcp_plugin.rs` built via [[bin]] or a separate `mock-mcp-plugin` bin crate under `crates/`) that responds to tools/list with a static payload; assert PluginManager::health_check returns a populated tools list.
Re-export incremental::{FileRevision, ...} from crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs and plugin_manager::{PluginManager, PluginManifest, PluginHealth} from crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs.
Per-crate rustdoc on every pub item added; `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` already present at crate roots must remain clean.

Commits follow .claude/rules/commit-style.md with Phase: 1, Feature: P1-W3-F04 / P1-W3-F05, Work-order: WO-0009 trailers. Module-introduction commits may exceed the ~50-line soft target per DEC-0001/DEC-0005 provided they are single-new-file type+impl+tests and would not compile if split.
Push after every commit — no hoarding.
scope_out: HOT/COLD plugin lifecycle (P1-W3-F06) — do NOT implement idle_timeout/auto-restart; leave a //! note but no code.
Wiring the incremental engine to the live file watcher (P1-W3-F02) — it depends on P1-W2-F06 which is in-flight via WO-0006; not this WO's responsibility.
Registering MCP tools on the daemon MCP server (P1-W3-F07) — separate WO.
Plugin crash-restart counters and circuit breakers — Phase 2 Week 6 scope.
Discovering real external plugins (Serena, ast-grep, semgrep) — Phase 1 Week 5 and Phase 2.
Any modification of tests/fixtures/**, ucil-build/feature-list.json, scripts/gate/**, scripts/flip-feature.sh, or ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md.
acceptance: P1-W3-F04: crates/ucil-core exposes an incremental engine skeleton with a tracked input and tracked query, and tests demonstrate invalidation + early cutoff.
P1-W3-F05: crates/ucil-daemon can discover a plugin.toml, spawn the plugin process, send JSON-RPC tools/list over stdio with a timeout, and return the parsed tool list.
No stubs, no todo!()/unimplemented!()/NotImplementedError, no mocks of salsa or of the plugin child process (use a real in-tree mock MCP binary instead).
All tests reachable from real entry points; mutation-check-worthy (reality-check.sh per-file rollback expected to fail on the new files).
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-core incremental:: --no-fail-fast && echo ok — exits 0 with at least 3 tests executed (memoise, invalidate-on-change, early-cutoff).
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager:: --no-fail-fast && echo ok — exits 0 with at least 3 tests executed (manifest parse, discover, spawn+health-check).
grep -RInE 'todo!\(|unimplemented!\(|NotImplementedError' crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs returns no matches.
grep -RInE '#\[ignore\]|\.skip\(' crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests returns no matches.
grep -l 'tokio::time::timeout' crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — every async IO path wraps await in a timeout per rust-style.md.
scripts/reality-check.sh WO-0009 exits 0 (per-file stash → tests fail → pop → tests pass for the two new modules).
context_refs: master-plan:§10 Daemon architecture (lines 137–145) — Salsa 2022 DAG, plugin manager lifecycle
master-plan:§14 Plugin system (lines 1443–1495) — plugin.toml schema, states, health_check = tools/list
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 3 — both features scoped together in the master plan week
.claude/rules/rust-style.md — tokio::time::timeout on IO awaits, thiserror per-crate Error enum, no .unwrap() outside cfg(test), rustdoc on pub items
decision:DEC-0001-WO-0002-large-commits.md — module-introduction commits may exceed soft 50-line target
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits.md — single-file coherent type+impl+#[cfg(test)] mod tests is an accepted exception
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate.md — cargo-mutants removed from per-WO verifier gate; reality-check.sh is the mutation oracle

lesson:WO-0006 test-selector mismatch — acceptance selectors here are prefix-style (`incremental::`, `plugin_manager::`) so nested #[cfg(test)] mod tests is safe, but any test name you put under it must still have its full path begin with the selector prefix; DO NOT rename the modules.

lesson:WO-0008 — mutation-gate 41% caused cap-rescue; now removed. Do NOT relax reality-check.sh coverage of the two new modules — every new pub fn must have at least one assertion that fails when the fn body is stashed.
planner_notes: Both features' deps (P0-W1-F02) are green on main. WO-0006 (P1-W2-F02/F03/F06) and WO-0008 (P1-W3-F01/P1-W4-F07) are in-verifier-queue and will flip on the next verifier cycle post DEC-0007; neither of their in-flight features is a dependency of this WO, so this WO can run in parallel with the pending re-verifications even under the serial executor model (DEC-0006) — it will simply be the next WO dequeued after the current verifier drains. No ADR required: the Salsa crate choice is specified in master-plan §10 line 137 (Salsa 2022, v0.25+) and the plugin.toml schema is specified in §14.1; the skeleton contract in this WO implements the minimum subset required by the frozen acceptance selectors.
